title: Inga houstonii
taxonomy: Inga houstonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Inga, which comprises about 300 species of trees and shrubs native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The exact classification of Inga houstonii at the species level is still being researched and may be subject to change. Further classification of the species is necessary to determine its exact place in the botanical hierarchy. Inga houstonii is a member of the tribe Ingeae, which is part of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae.
uses: Inga houstonii has been reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Inga houstonii is also used to treat fever and gastrointestinal issues in some parts of the world. The leaves and bark of the plant are often used in folk remedies. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Inga houstonii for medicinal purposes.
